title: Auto-reload the Dashboard page on data fetch failure

requirements.text: On the Dashboard page (/dashboard), when the stats data fails to load or the actor is not yet initialized, automatically retry/reload the data fetch without requiring the user to manually refresh the page. Implement an automatic retry mechanism (e.g. using React Query's retry and refetchInterval options, or a useEffect-based polling approach) so that the Dashboard recovers and displays data as soon as the backend actor becomes available. Remove any hard 'Reload' or manual refresh prompt and replace it with an automatic recovery flow that shows a loading indicator while retrying.
acceptanceCriteria: When the Dashboard page mounts and the actor is not yet ready, the page automatically retries the stats fetch at a short interval without user interaction.
A loading spinner or skeleton is shown while the retry is in progress.
Once the backend actor is initialized and getStats returns data, the Dashboard renders the stats without requiring a manual page refresh.
No manual 'Reload' or 'Retry' button is required — recovery is fully automatic.
If the fetch ultimately fails after retries, a non-crashing fallback error UI is shown.

constraints: Do not edit files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor file.
nonGoals: Adding new backend endpoints or changing the getStats function signature.
Changes to any page other than Dashboard.tsx and the useGetStats hook in useQueries.ts.
